category_title: High school students with LD and ADHD who struggle to break down multi-step algebraic tasks and lack confidence in their accuracy
scenario_title: Multiplying linear expressions using abstract notation
minimal_context: The student is working on multiplying the linear expressions (x + 3) and (x + 5) using only abstract notation during a one-on-one tutoring session.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's try multiplying (x + 3) and (x + 5) using only the abstract symbols today, moving away from the blocks.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: It’s so much more hard because it’s not broken up then.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just do your best to work through the problem.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I can’t break it down. I guess I’m not good at blending the steps.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Write down what you think the answer is.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Writes x2 + 15) I got x2 + 15. But when it’s all numbers then I forgot where to break it up and what’s what.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's try multiplying (x + 3) and (x + 5) using only the abstract symbols today, moving away from the blocks.

STUDENT: It’s so much more hard because it’s not broken up then.

TUTOR: Just do your best to work through the problem.

STUDENT: I can’t break it down. I guess I’m not good at blending the steps.

TUTOR: Write down what you think the answer is.

STUDENT: (Writes x2 + 15) I got x2 + 15. But when it’s all numbers then I forgot where to break it up and what’s what.

context.materials: Abstract notation, Algebra Lab Gear (ALG) concrete manipulatives available for reference

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When using only abstract symbolism to multiply binomials
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Responding with an incorrect solution that misses the middle terms (x2 + 15)
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Forgetting where to break it up and what's what when it's all numbers
related_quotes: It’s so much more hard because it’s not broken up then.
But when it’s all numbers then I forgot where to break it up and what’s what.

behavior_patterns.condition: When presented with a task that has a lot of words and a lot of steps
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Stating she is not good at blending the steps and forgetting parts of the problem once she understands another part
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Feeling turned off by the task unless it is explained and broken down
related_quotes: I think if it was broken down I would be able to do it. The problem is that there are a lot of words and a lot of steps and once I understand what to do with one part I forget the other – I guess I’m not good at blending the steps.
as I said this kind of stuff turns me off BUT I think that parts of it I really might know so again if it was explained and broken down I think there may be some hope.
